message:
feat(search): add two-tier quality coverage health reporting and deterministic fallback vectors

The two-tier semantic index previously stored all-zero quality embedding
rows when quality embeddings were unavailable, which caused two problems:
(1) quality_search and quality_scores_for_indices would include these
zero-vector documents in results, producing misleading similarity scores,
and (2) the all-zero rows wasted memory without contributing to search
quality.

This commit addresses both issues:

- TwoTierIndex::search_quality and quality_scores_for_indices now skip
  documents where has_quality is false, ensuring only genuinely embedded
  documents participate in quality-tier scoring.

- TwoTierBridge::upsert_document now generates a deterministic non-zero
  fallback vector (derived from the fast embedding with small offsets)
  instead of storing all-zero rows. The has_quality flag remains false,
  so the fallback preserves index-shape invariants without polluting
  quality search results.

- A new TwoTierIndexingHealth struct exposes quality coverage metrics
  (total docs, quality doc count, coverage ratio/percent, dimensions)
  through the two_tier_indexing_health() function, wired into the
  health check response via identity.rs.

- Robot mode thread rendering uses # instead of ## for the top-level
  heading, improving markdown hierarchy in agent-consumed output.

- Comprehensive tests cover fallback determinism, empty-input edge cases,
  quality search filtering, quality score exclusion, and health reporting
  accuracy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
